item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to echo Kalinin and Petrograd'a sentiments above. It's an interesting concept, and I want to like it. Unfortunately, the redacted information just takes me right out of the narrative. It's a bit excessive, and it just doesn't work for me.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for making the change; haven't been on the site since posting this and didn't notice the issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[DATA EXPURGED]

I don't know precisely what caused that, but :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never mind, just shrunk it to 300px and 22.73 KB.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just scale it down to 300px or 600px wide and save as a jpeg. That'll do the trick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I created and uploaded a smaller one, but there's a draft saved in the history, so I don't feel safe editing the page. Hopefully the author will see this.

Edit: Ugh, the one I did is still huge… file sizes are such a pain. I'll do it again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could someone please reduce the size of the image on this article? That fucker just cost me 10.5 MB. :@

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
***Feels bad thinking of how many times made trucks attempt to drive across bridges made of nothing but cable. Curiously the default bridge looked quite a bit like the picture here…

Anyways, too much redaction in the last bit. Imagining a site-wide containment breach caused by someone out there with a rogue copy tickled my funny bone more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is interesting, but the test logs could be longer and the Shepherd stuff feels too [REDACTED] to tell an actual story

Neutral vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only who saw the fact that it erased soil from existence in one test and thought "potentially effective means of erasing problematic objects from existence?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The extent to which Procedure 371-Shepherd is redacted…. a) it's a nice touch of realism, but b) it's uninteresting, which is a greater sin than unrealism in this context. Unless you do something better with 371-Shepherd (which could include 'removing most of it'), I can't upvote.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Thank you, that is correct. I chose to use -2' as this would be read 'two prime' - i.e. the 'proper' version.

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 5 of this article was reverted, as it corrected something that (presumably) wasn't an error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had the biggest engineering nerd grin on my face the whole time I was reading this. So much +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy this. The Scranton anchors are a bit extraneous, since the [DATA EXPUNGED] doesn't really connect it to the reality problems below. In fact, the entire reality problem is weakened by the amount of removal. I think it should remain, but with more details and less vague expungement.

Overall, however, I really like the concept of a real-life design program and the Foundation using it as a backup. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mostly agreeing with Kalinin above. Your clinical tone and format are good. The idea of anomalous CAD creating real objects is… ok. The majority of the article is a pretty basic exploration of that, and never gets particularly weird or interesting.

The Shepard protocol… the base idea of using this as a rapid containment center is nifty, even if you don't explain how they got things which had breached containment to -2's location, which is kind of an important point.

Really, though, with all the redaction, you haven't told that story at all. I appreciate the narrative ploy that we don't have clearance, that's a fine idea, but it's too much here. We need details for this to work. I'd be much more interested if you cross-linked to some lesser-known SCPs and told a good story with them than the blank space we have here.

-1

item.title: Re: First SCP
item.content:encoded: Please reduce its size for your use here. You're currently forcing people to download over 10 MB of image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The part where the redactions were bugging me was when I got the Shepard protocol. I wondered to myself what's the point of even having this section at all if every other word or so was blacked out or redacted. "In Universe" I understand why it's like that, but as a reader I was annoyed.

I still liked it overall, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cleaning up the redaction (I don't think all of it is bad, mind, but there's too much here to detract from the article) would improve this, but even with that cleaned up, this isn't interesting or entertaining enough to warrant an up-vote from me. I really start to lose any interest I still had by "Unauthorised Incident 075".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for taking the time to explain why you didn't like it! You raise some good points. My intentions with all the redaction was to imply that the reader wasn't that highly ranked within the Foundation, but on reflection I'd agree that it leaves out too much. Will think about some potential changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I probably would have liked this better as just an anomalous shareware CAD program. I'm not against the concept of larger implications being explored related to the anomaly, but the way in which it was done here was a cautionary example in bad expungement, starting with the experiment log:

Test #: 016
Description: A battery of Scranton reality anchors were established around SCP-2787-2. Various models were saved including repeats of previous tests.
Result: [REDACTED]

Something involving Scranton anchors1 happened. That's it.

Test #: 017
Description: Extensive changes to model in attempt to [REDACTED] and simulation of containment failure of SCP-████.

An attempt to [REDACTED]. Okay.

Result: [REDACTED] leading to use of SCP-2787 for emergency containment being approved by O5 Council majority vote

So something happened and then it led to something else happening. Somehow this results in a brand new Thaumiel object for some reason. Maybe this sounds like a glib characterization, but this is what you're giving the reader.

The last addendum is just as bad. Some other skip, with no clues as to what it is or what it does (except for "bend reality" I guess), causes something bad to happen, and then something else happens. And now we can't use SCP-2787 any more, except when something bad (how bad, we're not sure, because that's redacted too) happens. The end.

This isn't a narrative. It's not even the outline of a narrative. This is, maybe, the framework for a standard SCP article, with all the details that would make such an entry interesting yet to be filled in, almost entirely. The only detail you provide, that it's tied to an anomalous CAD program, is the part of the article you spend the least time exploring.

This is coasting by on so much vagueness and abuse of redaction that I feel like, as a reader, I'm being conned. Downvoted with no hesitation.

Footnotes
1. This is, by the way, exactly what I was concerned about when the whole Scranton thing was put forward. Handwavey magic reality thing used to explain the mysterious instead of writing about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm literally in a CAD class as of the time reading this so I already like the idea. I also like the idea of this being used as a thaumiel. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like that you acknowledge that the reader does not have level 5 clearance for the protocol procedures.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm upvoting off of potential, as the idea is interesting but the execution needs work.

item.title: First SCP
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP! Image is from Wikimedia and is under a Creative Commons attribution/share-alike licence, author is 'MaureenvdB'

+ show block
– hide block

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Single_lane_steel_bridge.JPG
